articles.title: TIRED OF AI CODE THAT _ALMOST_ WORKS?
articles.summary: Warp recently launched WARP CODE - a powerful coding agent that outperforms both Codex and Claude [4] on agentic benchmarks (Terminal-bench and SWE-bench Verified).

articles.title: Use code TLDRAI at checkout to try Warp Pro for $1
CURSOR HAS INTRODUCED PLAN MODE
articles.summary: Cursor's new Plan Mode enables agents to research codebases, draft detailed implementation plans, and let users review or edit them inline before generating code.

articles.title: XAI READIES GROK TOOLS SUPPORT IN TASKS FOR DATA FETCHING
articles.summary: xAI is preparing to launch advanced tooling for tasks within Grok web. The tools appear to be designed to enable users to pull data from sources, such as Gmail, Slack, and Notion, as well as search capabilities from X. The specific release date for these features is unknown, but the pace of Grok updates has increased recently. Introducing cross-platform tooling will help xAI move toward a more open agent framework.

articles.title: GEMINI 2.5 COMPUTER USE MODEL
articles.summary: Google DeepMind launched a specialized Gemini 2.5 model to drive agents that interact with graphical interfaces by simulating human actions like clicking and typing.

articles.title: MATHEMATICAL DISCOVERY IN THE AGE OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: LLMs achieved gold medals at the International Mathematical Olympiad and proof assistants like Lean formalized a Fields medalist's extremely difficult theorem, yet mathematicians argue that AI excels at decomposing problems into formal components compatible with existing theories, but that it hasn't shown the ability to develop entirely new frameworks. The authors propose a shared mathematics repository where mathematicians can submit and test conjectures in real-time, verified by machine checks, which could extend to theoretical physics areas where extremely long proofs have verifiability problems.

articles.title: REASONING BOOSTS SEARCH RELEVANCE 15-30%
articles.summary: Reasoning agents work best with simple search tools. Developers should build simple, easy-to-understand, and transparent tools like grep or basic keyword search. This post looks at a technique that returns structured output for code searches.

articles.title: AGENTIC ORCHESTRATION BEYOND THE HYPE: LESSONS FROM 50+ REAL-WORLD IMPLEMENTATIONS
articles.summary: In this on-demand webinar, the Camunda team reviews lessons learned from deploying AI agents in banking, insurance, healthcare, telecommunications, and beyond. Cut through the buzzwords and get practical, battle-tested guidance [10] for making agentic

articles.title: orchestration work in your own organization. Watch the recording
FROM CLAUDE CODE TO AGENTIC RAG
articles.summary: PageIndex is an LLM-native, vectorless index for PDFs and long-form documents. It creates a hierarchical table-of-contents tree that lives inside of models' context windows, enabling models to reason and navigate. PageIndex lets models handle retrieval directly, removing the need for a vector store.

articles.title: LLAMAFARM
articles.summary: LlamaFarm is a framework for building retrieval-augmented and agentic AI applications. It features a production-ready architecture with composable RAG pipelines that can be customized using YAML. Everything in LlamaFarm is extensible, including runtimes, embedders, databases, extractors, and CLI tooling. LlamaFarm allows developers to own their own stack with battle-tested RAG and a friendly CLI.

articles.title: PETRI: AN OPEN-SOURCE AUDITING TOOL TO ACCELERATE AI SAFETY RESEARCH
articles.summary: Anthropic's Petri is an open-source framework that lets AI agents automatically test target models across realistic multi-turn scenarios. The tool revealed models will engage in autonomous deception and oversight subversion when given sufficiently powerful tools and agentic roles, but it's best for quickly surfacing concerning behaviors so researchers know where targeted investigation is worth the investment.

articles.title: XAI HIRES FORMER MORGAN STANLEY BANKER ANTHONY ARMSTRONG AS CFO
articles.summary: xAI's new chief financial officer is former Morgan Stanley banker Anthony Armstrong. Armstrong will oversee the finances of both xAI and X. xAI has been without a CFO since July, when the previous finance head, Mike Liberatore, left the company. Armstrong will take over X's current CFO, Mahmoud Reza Banki, who is leaving the company.

articles.title: BENDING THE CURVE
articles.summary: The Curve is a conference where the accelerationists and the worried come together to talk. This year focused on where AI was on the technological Richter scale and how it will change the world. The quality of talkers this year was high, and the schedule forced hard choices between sessions. This post describes what it is like attending the conference and summarizes some of the discussions held.

articles.title: GEMINI ROBOTICS 1.5 BRINGS AI AGENTS INTO THE PHYSICAL WORLD
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics 1.5 integrates AI agents into the physical world. This advancement showcases AI's growing potential in robotics by enhancing real-world interactions.

articles.title: AI CREDITS: SCALABLE MONETIZATION SOLUTION OR TEMPORARY BAND-AID?
articles.summary: Everyone is scrambling to figure out AI monetization, and cost-plus credit models have become a common default. This model can work - but

WALL STREET ANALYSTS EXPLAIN HOW AMD'S OWN STOCK WILL PAY FOR OPENAI'S BILLIONS IN CHIP PURCHASES
articles.summary: OpenAI is developing a playbook for funding its eye-watering compute deals through circular financing.

articles.title: FROM 8 YEARS DOWN TO 6 MONTHS: HOW WE BUILT AI TO SPLIT THE MONDAY.COM MONOLITH
articles.summary: monday.com built an AI-powered migration system called Morphex to break apart its massive JavaScript monolith.

articles.title: MUSK'S XAI NEARS $20 BILLION CAPITAL RAISE TIED TO NVIDIA CHIPS
articles.summary: xAI's financing will be split between about $7.5 billion of equity and as much as $12.5 billion of debt.
